evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Use big data tools and platforms to create and maintain data pipelines, making sure pipelines are robust, scalable, and reliable
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Rigour signals (robust, scalable, reliable pipelines; own data quality and SLA) dominate with no velocity/speed language present.
evidence.domain_risk: Understand business processes, applications and how data is created, stored and gathered in Singapore Airlines Group
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Major airline sector could imply high risk, but the JD language is generic IT/data-team framing with no explicit financial, regulatory, or safety-compliance language, placing it at moderate risk per the tie-breaker rule.
evidence.data_team_maturity: Design, build and manage data marts to satisfy our growing data needs
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Named responsibilities (data marts, SLA ownership, big data platforms) and "growing data needs" phrasing indicate a data function that exists and is scaling.
evidence.jd_authorship: Experience working with AWS, GCP, and infrastructure-as-code systems
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Specific cloud platforms, ETL pipeline work, and Tableau visualization requirements give a concrete picture of the daily work.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Design, build and manage data marts to satisfy our growing data needs
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role centres on internal data marts, pipeline ownership, and business insight generation for internal consumers rather than a specific external-facing function.
evidence.autonomy_level: Build expertise and domain knowledge on the data. Establish and own the data quality and SLA
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Establishing and owning data quality/SLA is strategic, while being "part of a project team to manage and deliver project deliverables" is execution-oriented, giving mixed autonomy.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No AI tool or AI infrastructure mention appears anywhere in the extracted JD text.
evidence.testing_framing: Establish and own the data quality and SLA
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Data quality and SLA ownership is framed as a named, owned responsibility with an explicit ownership verb, not just a tool in a skill list.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: making sure pipelines are robust, scalable, and reliable
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Pipeline reliability and SLA ownership are a stated operational concern, but the JD does not raise compliance, regulatory, or stakeholder-trust framing.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Design, build and manage data marts to satisfy our growing data needs
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The role mixes building new data marts/pipelines with maintaining and managing existing ones as data needs grow.
evidence.loss_aversion: making sure pipelines are robust, scalable, and reliable — reflects concern for pipeline stability and SLA adherence rather than compliance or regulatory risk.
